page.title: Inflammatory Bowel Disease — Disease Intelligence | OSMF
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Inflammatory Bowel Disease: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Inflammatory Bowel Disease: 178 alterations and 200 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Indigo naturalis is effective in the treatment of ulcerative colitis.
Herbal medicines for the treatment of active ulcerative colitis.
High intake of ultra-processed foods was linked with an enhanced inflammatory bowel disease risk.
Pharmacological effects of berberine on models of ulcerative colitis.
Protective effects of ginsenosides on ulcerative colitis.
Resveratrol for inflammatory bowel disease in preclinical studies.
Safety and efficacy of curcumin in the treatment of ulcerative colitis.
Cesarean delivery is associated with celiac disease in children.
Dietary probiotics are effective in improving the symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
Efficacy and safety of adjuvant curcumin therapy in ulcerative colitis
Efficacy of vitamin D in treatment of inflammatory bowel disease.
Inflammatory bowel disease is significantly associated with having higher odds of vitamin D deficiency.
Rates of inflammatory bowel disease with onset in childhood are moderately increased after birth by cesarean section.
Sucrose intake was found positively related with ulcerative colitis risk.
Therapeutic effects of epigallocatechin-3-gallate for inflammatory bowel disease.
There is an increased risk for inflammatory bowel disease in oral contraceptive agent users.
This meta-analysis indicated that the intake of dietary fiber was significantly associated with a decreased risk of inflammatory bowel disease.
Use of curcumin in achieving clinical and endoscopic remission in ulcerative colitis.
Vitamin D levels are lower in IBD patients, suggesting that vitamin D plays an important role in the pathogenesis of IBD.
Vitamin D therapy in adults with inflammatory bowel disease.
The intestinal microbial metabolite acetyl l-carnitine improves gut inflammation and immune homeostasis via CADM2.
Efficacy of Indigo naturalis therapy for ulcerative colitis.
A combination of lactobacillus reuteri and lactobacillus rhamnosus reduces inflammation in patients with Crohn's disease or ulcerative colitis.
A Frankincense extract attenuates the symptoms associated with mild Ulcerative Colitis in remission.
A prebiotic and anthocyanin blend improved gastrointestinal health and impacted skin status in humans.
A simplified yoga-based regimen is a safe and effective complementary clinical treatment modality for patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
A steamed broccoli sprout diet preparation that reduces colitis via the gut microbiota.
Addition of curcumin to mesalamine therapy was superior to placebo and mesalamine in inducing clinical and endoscopic remission in patients with mild-to-moderate active UC.
Administration of polyphenon E resulted in a therapeutic benefit for patients who were refractory to 5-aminosalicylic and/or azathioprine.
Air pollution and risk of 32 health conditions: outcome-wide analyses in a population-based prospective cohort.
Aloe vera gel has therapeutic potential in the treatment of inflammatory bowel diseases.
An anthocyanin-rich bilberry extract is a promising therapeutic approach for the treatment of IBD.
An exclusion diet and nutraceutical therapy has significant therapeutic effects in juvenile Crohn's disease.
An oral supplement enriched with fish oil, FOS, soluble fiber, gum arabic and antioxidants reduce the need for corticosteroids in ulcerative colitis.
Ascorbic acid may modify the composition of gut microbiota which plays a role in the pathogenesis of an inflammatory bowel disease.
Bifidobacterium infantis and Lactobacillus acidophilus reduces the incidence and severity of necrotizing enterocolitis in very low birth weight infants.
Boswellia appears superior to mesalazine in safety and efficacy in the treatment of Crohn's disease.
Boswellia demonstrates significant effect against Ulcerative colitis.
Cannabis appears to have some therapeutic value (based on self-reporting) amongst patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
Cannabis use by inflammatory bowel disease patients can induce clinical improvement and is associated with reduced use of medication and slight weight gain.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Disease course unchanged in ~85% of patients at 10 years on standard care; ~23% (CD) and ~12% (UC) relapse within 12 months of thiopurine withdrawal in sustained remission
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Anti-IL-23 biologics (mirikizumab, risankizumab) maintain steroid-free remission in >60% at 2 years — but on the drug, not drug-free
remission.gap_size: Large -- but between 'no remission' and 'on-drug remission'; drug-free remission remains rare
remission.barrier_detail: Biologic revolution has raised on-drug remission without meaningfully improving drug-free remission rates; true disease modification (cure-like durability off treatment) remains elusive
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — IL-23 inhibitors added to UC/CD ladder; 2018-2022 JAK inhibitors (tofacitinib, upadacitinib) added; anti-TNF (infliximab) first-line since 1998, essentially unchanged as backbone
remission.outdated_treatments: Long-term corticosteroids as maintenance (bone density loss, metabolic effects; guidelines explicitly recommend against steroid-dependent maintenance); azathioprine monotherapy without biologic in moderate-severe disease
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT) — RCT evidence in UC showing ~32% remission vs 9% placebo; exclusive enteral nutrition (EEN) in pediatric Crohn's comparable to steroids; low-FODMAP diet; exclusive dietary therapy trials underway
remission.frontier_signal: FMT (multiple Phase 3 underway); stem cell transplantation for refractory CD (ASTIC trial); selective JAK1 inhibitors (filgotinib, upadacitinib); gut-selective S1P modulators; anti-TL1A (tulisokibart, Phase 3)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
